headline: AWS puts $1B into new AI unit to embed engineers with customers
detail: This is a direct signal that hyperscaler AI spending is accelerating, not slowing. That benefits the entire semiconductor and cloud stack - CRWD, SMH, SOXL, and QQQ all have meaningful exposure to AI infrastructure demand. Sentiment lift for your whole book.
rec: If CRWD pulls into scanner entry $740-$744 (stop $731), that's a legitimate spot to add to your existing position given the AI tailwind confirmed today. Don't chase it at $764.

headline: Japanese yen sinks to 40-year low
detail: Yen weakness at extremes raises the real risk of Bank of Japan intervention, which historically triggers a fast unwind of yen-carry trades - money that's been funding risk assets including US tech. Not an immediate crisis but worth watching; your margin book is exposed if carry unwinds sharply.
rec: No action today, but keep it on radar. If yen intervention headlines hit, expect volatility in QQQ and SMH first - those are your cleanest hedges to trim if things move fast.

headline: Trump's massive defense budget sparks state business battles
detail: Large defense spending shifts capital and political attention but has limited direct impact on your tech/chip names unless defense-adjacent semis (think radar, comms chips) get contract news. No specific catalyst for your holdings from this headline alone.
rec: Nothing in your book has a clean direct link to this headline today. Hold what you have.

headline: French biotech soars 40% on experimental medicine data
detail: Purely a biotech-specific move with zero overlap to your portfolio. Mentioned only to flag that spec momentum is alive in markets today, which is mild background support for your smaller spec names like PTNM - but not enough to override a broken chart.
rec: Don't let a biotech pop change your discipline on PTNM. The stop is $9.50 for a reason; a rising speculative mood elsewhere doesn't fix a position that's already down 15%.
